text: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments. [Sector forecast](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/index.html). [Healthcare](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/index.html). 05/11/2025 • 6 minutes reading. [David Bolscher](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/david-bolscher/index.html). The healthcare sector will grow by 3 percent this year and by 3.5 percent in 2026, ABN AMRO expects. This growth gives healthcare providers financial space for necessary investments in outdated real estate and innovative healthcare services. This means that healthcare providers can also provide good care if the next government decides to make cuts. The healthcare sector already showed a significant improvement in results last year thanks to postponed government cuts and improved employability of healthcare providers. In 2024, healthcare providers achieved an average return of 2.4 percent, according to figures from consultancy EY. In 2023 the result was on average 1.4 percent of turnover and in 2022 only 1 percent.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: ABN AMRO expects that healthcare providers will also achieve good returns in 2025 and 2026.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Thanks to the government's ample budget, volumes are expected to grow by 3 percent this year and by 3.5 percent in 2026. A number of rates that providers receive are also rising faster than inflation. That is good news, because the generally tight margins of healthcare providers leave little room for investment. Due to the lack of investments in healthcare real estate and innovation, healthcare providers rely heavily on expensive staff. For example, figures from the Central Bureau of Statistics (CBS) show that staff deployment in the healthcare sector has increased by more than 17 percent since 2017, while the increase in other sectors was limited to 13 percent. Tightness on the labor market and high personnel costs will slow down the growth in personnel deployment in the coming years, meaning that healthcare providers will mainly have to rely on increasing labor productivity. In addition, the government will probably want to slow down rising healthcare expenditure from 2027 to find room for other expenditures such as defense, according to the calculations of party programs by the Central Planning Bureau (CPB).

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: The question is whether all savings are feasible.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: For example, freezing the basic package seems unrealistic.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: However, a new cabinet may decide to abolish the halving of the deductible and to cut back on elderly care. Despite a shortage of staff and possible cutbacks, healthcare providers can continue to provide good care with efficient business operations and increasing labor productivity.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: The relatively good results in recent and coming years offer room to invest in innovation and real estate.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: With the use of innovative tools, care providers have to perform fewer care tasks and the expansion of digital systems reduces the administrative burden.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Renovated real estate can alleviate healthcare tasks, attract patients and staff, and reduce maintenance and energy costs.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Significant volume growth in long-term care. Long-term care is expected to grow by 5 percent next year. The growth is due to increases in the government budget for care for the disabled, mental health care (ggz) and elderly care. In disability care and mental health care, additional resources should mainly contribute to lower waiting lists and improved quality of care. In elderly care, the larger budget must provide care for more elderly people. To achieve growth, healthcare providers must be able to attract sufficient patients and clients in addition to sufficient staff. Typically, the demand for care is not an inhibiting factor if government budgets allow more growth. Yet nursing homes often struggle with empty rooms due to lagging demand. The occupancy of nursing home beds has fallen slightly in the past year. A survey among healthcare providers shows that outdated nursing homes in particular have difficulty attracting sufficient residents. When nursing homes are renovated and made more sustainable, they will probably be full again. When renovating, it is important to take into account the wishes of the elderly and the emergence of new forms of residential care, as described in our previous analysis on [the abolition of intensive home care](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/minder-zorg-voor-kwetsbare-ouderen.html). Nursing home providers can also temporarily rent out vacant spaces as independent senior housing. Higher rates for care and real estate in long-term care. In addition to volume growth, healthcare providers in long-term care can count on a good result next year. Previously announced rate cuts have largely been canceled, although the rates that healthcare providers receive for elderly care are rising slightly less rapidly than inflation. After cost price research, the rates for disability care and mental health care are actually rising faster than inflation. Healthcare providers can on average count on an additional rate increase of 2.5 percent. In addition to the increase in healthcare rates, the reimbursement for real estate in long-term care has also been adjusted upwards by 13 percent. The increase in real estate compensation compensates for the increased construction costs for healthcare providers. Healthcare providers in long-term care can use the increase to realize their construction plans. Growth in general practitioner care should prevent expensive care. Primary care also has room for 5 percent volume growth next year. The growth is mainly driven by an increase in general practitioner care and community nursing. Primary care providers are designated in care agreements to meet the increasing care demand of an aging population as much as possible. More financial resources will be used to ensure an adequate supply of primary care to reduce treatments at more expensive hospitals. The challenge for growth mainly lies in the limited influx of healthcare providers. The ample government budget makes more healthcare services in primary care possible. For example, from 2026, health insurers will reimburse so-called thoughtful advice. The GP coordinates with a medical specialist from a hospital what care a patient needs, so that a referral to the hospital is prevented.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Small hospitals fear volume standards. Hospital care can count on the least growth of all healthcare sectors. Health insurers that purchase hospital care aim for fewer interventions and try to prevent cost increases. Smaller hospitals in particular therefore have difficulty achieving a positive result. Their financial situation may deteriorate further in the coming years because healthcare purchasers apply volume standards to accelerate the concentration of care. With volume standards, hospitals are only allowed to perform a certain treatment if they perform that treatment often enough in a year. Hospitals must hand over treatments that do not meet the volume standard to other hospitals. Hospitals that lose treatments will in principle receive patients back for less complex treatments to cushion the loss of turnover.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: The question is whether this exchange actually takes place in practice.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: In addition, small hospitals fear that doctors will leave due to a narrower treatment offering. In the long run, this will also affect the turnover of these hospitals. What will stimulate turnover in the coming years is the intended introduction of budget financing for emergency care, which will mainly benefit smaller hospitals. Healthcare providers see economies of scale for growth. Healthcare providers in other healthcare sectors are also trying to achieve economies of scale.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Last year, the number of approved applications for mergers and acquisitions in healthcare rose to 214, according to figures from the Dutch Healthcare Authority (NZa). Such forms of scaling up are only reported there for approval if at least one of the organizations involved employs more than 50 healthcare providers.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Healthcare providers want to strengthen their market share through mergers and acquisitions. A larger market share strengthens the position of healthcare providers in contract negotiations with healthcare purchasers.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: In addition, administration and other support services can be carried out more efficiently.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Healthcare providers also indicate that a merger or acquisition gives them more financial options for digitalization and renovation of real estate. Commercial chains are increasingly acquiring small providers to achieve economies of scale in healthcare segments where profit can be paid out.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: For example, dental care had the most concentrations last year, with 62 takeover applications to the NZa. Dentists who sell their practice mainly give the reason that they are about to retire. Other dentists often mention the realization of synergy benefits as a reason for sales.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: After oral care, the most concentrations last year were in long-term care. In 2024, long-term care submitted 41 applications to the NZa, a doubling of 2023. In long-term care, this also concerns foundations that merge to keep care affordable and accessible in the future.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: Five organizations were taken over due to financial necessity.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: In addition to the benefits, a larger scale can make it difficult for healthcare providers to attract and retain staff.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text: For example, independent entrepreneurship disappears when a healthcare chain buys up a healthcare practice. The care provider who previously owned the practice may then no longer be willing to work the same number of hours, or may report sick more often. Healthcare providers that scale up must take the wishes of staff into account, so that takeovers actually lead to more accessible care. Read more in the healthcare sector. In the coming years, the sector will change to provide more care with fewer resources. The demand for care continues to increase due to an aging population, while it is hardly possible to attract more healthcare personnel.

metadata.source_title: Healthcare providers are given room for necessary investments

text:
Innovation and collaboration make work easier and more fun, ensuring that healthcare provision remains sustainable and of high quality. [View all articles](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/healthcare/index.html)
